title: rubus costatus var. rhamnifolius
intro: Rubus costatus var. rhamnifolius, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Rubus genus, characterized by its unique and distinct rhamnifolius leaf morphology. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, which are said to aid in wound healing and the treatment of various health conditions. The health benefits of Rubus costatus var. rhamnifolius include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and provide antioxidant protection against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Rubus costatus var. rhamnifolius can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to support overall health and well-being.
